name: CASE 1.1 Framework Graph Dictionary
plain_description: A structured dictionary for the platform's CASE framework graph model. It describes standards frameworks, framework items, associations, definitions, rubrics, and status fields exposed as SQL and JSON.
model_scope: Framework import, search, graph traversal, standards alignment, rubric metadata, and cross-standard linking. This is not a formal CASE certification claim.

shared_enums.name: Adoption Status
shared_enums.plain_description: Global allowed-value crosswalk for adoption status values across projected standards.

shared_enums.name: Association Type
shared_enums.plain_description: Global allowed-value crosswalk for association type values across projected standards.

objects.name: CASE Package
objects.plain_description: The full bundle for one framework: root document, items, relationship edges, definitions, and rubrics.
objects.why_it_exists: A package lets the platform import and version a complete standards framework as one unit.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this CASE package.
The root framework document in this package.
The standards publisher, state agency, institution, or importer that supplied the package.
A URI for the package or import source when one is available.
When the platform imported this CASE package.
Whether the CASE package passed platform validation.

objects.name: CASE Framework Document
objects.plain_description: The root document for a standards, competency, course-code, or skill framework.
objects.why_it_exists: Apps need a framework-level record before they can browse items, align content, or report mastery.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this framework document.
The official CASE UUID for this framework document.
A web identifier for this framework document.
What kind of framework this is.
The CASE version used by the document.
The organization with authority over the framework.
The name of the framework document.
A summary of what the framework covers.
Subject labels such as Mathematics, Reading, or Biology.
Default language used in the framework text.
The publisher's revision or version label.
The publication or adoption status of the framework.
A link to the human-readable official source document.
A link to legal permissions for using the framework.
When the publisher last changed this framework document.

objects.name: CASE Framework Item
objects.plain_description: One node in the framework, such as a standard, competency, skill, objective, course code, strand, domain, or cluster.
objects.why_it_exists: Framework items are the targets that QTI, OneRoster, Caliper, Common Cartridge, Open Badges, and CLR data align to.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this framework item.
The framework document this item belongs to.
The official CASE UUID for this framework item.
A web identifier for this framework item.
The short code people see in standards documents.
The complete text of the standard, competency, skill, objective, or grouping statement.
fields.school_example: Interpret and compute quotients of fractions.

fields.plain_description: A shortened version of the full statement.
Another label the publisher uses for this kind of item.
Publisher label for what kind of item this is.
Linked-data identifier for the item type.
Topic keywords connected to the item.
Subject labels for this item.
Intended grade, education level, or instructional level.
The item's visible position in a list or outline.
License that applies to this item.
When this item last changed.

objects.name: CASE Association
objects.plain_description: A relationship edge between framework documents or items.
objects.why_it_exists: Associations represent hierarchy, crosswalks, replacements, translations, sequence, and other framework relationships.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this association.
The framework document this association belongs to.
The official CASE UUID for this relationship.
A web identifier for this relationship.
What kind of relationship this is.
The item or document where the relationship starts.
The item or document where the relationship points.
The display or processing order for this relationship.
A group label or URI for related associations.
Publisher notes about the relationship.
When this association last changed.

objects.name: CASE Definition Set
objects.plain_description: A set of reusable lookup definitions used by a framework.
objects.why_it_exists: Definitions explain framework concepts, subjects, item types, licenses, and relationship groupings.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this definition set.
The framework document this definition set belongs to.
How many concept definitions are present.
How many subject definitions are present.
How many item type definitions are present.
How many license definitions are present.

objects.name: CASE Concept
objects.plain_description: A topic or idea addressed by the framework.
objects.why_it_exists: Concepts support search, browsing, tagging, and crosswalks across frameworks.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this concept.
The framework document this concept belongs to.
The official CASE UUID for the concept.
A web identifier for the concept.
The concept name.
Keywords connected to the concept.
The publisher's code showing where the concept fits in a concept hierarchy.
Explanation of the concept.
fields.school_example: Understanding parts of a whole and ratios.

fields.plain_description: When the concept last changed.

objects.name: CASE Subject
objects.plain_description: An academic or topical subject used by the framework.
objects.why_it_exists: Subjects let developers filter frameworks and standards by broad learning area.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this subject.
The framework document this subject belongs to.
The official CASE UUID for the subject.
A web identifier for the subject.
The subject name.
The publisher's code for where the subject fits in a subject hierarchy.
Explanation of the subject.
When the subject last changed.

objects.name: CASE Item Type
objects.plain_description: A definition explaining what kind of framework item something is.
objects.why_it_exists: Item types tell developers whether a node is a domain, strand, standard, competency, skill, objective, or course code.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this item type.
The framework document this item type belongs to.
The official CASE UUID for the item type.
A web identifier for the item type.
The item type name.
Explanation of the item type.
fields.school_example: A statement describing what students should know or do.

fields.plain_description: The publisher's code for where the item type fits in a hierarchy.
A normalized item type code used by the platform.
When the item type last changed.

objects.name: CASE License
objects.plain_description: A definition of permissions for using a framework, item, rubric, or related content.
objects.why_it_exists: Apps need to know whether standards text and related content may be displayed, copied, or reused.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this license.
The framework document this license belongs to.
The official CASE UUID for the license record.
A web identifier for the license record.
The license name.
Explanation of the license.
The legal text or a reference to legal text for the license.
fields.school_example: See the linked Creative Commons license.

fields.plain_description: When the license record last changed.

objects.name: CASE Association Grouping
objects.plain_description: A label for a set of associations so people can understand why those relationships belong together.
objects.why_it_exists: Groupings help explain batches of crosswalks, hierarchy edges, or mapping relationships.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this association grouping.
The framework document this grouping belongs to.
The official CASE UUID for the association group.
A web identifier for the association group.
The group name.
Explanation of the group.
fields.school_example: Parent-child relationships used for the official framework outline.

fields.plain_description: When the group last changed.

objects.name: CASE Rubric
objects.plain_description: A rubric describing criteria and performance levels for judging work or mastery.
objects.why_it_exists: Rubrics connect standards to scoring guidance, performance levels, and feedback.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this rubric.
The framework document this rubric belongs to.
The official CASE UUID for the rubric.
A web identifier for the rubric.
The rubric name.
Explanation of the rubric.
fields.school_example: Evaluates explanation, strategy, accuracy, and representation.

fields.plain_description: When the rubric last changed.

objects.name: CASE Rubric Criterion
objects.plain_description: One thing being judged in a rubric, such as reasoning, fluency, accuracy, evidence, or collaboration.
objects.why_it_exists: Criteria let apps show and analyze the parts of a rubric separately.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this rubric criterion.
The rubric this criterion belongs to.
The official CASE UUID for the criterion.
A web identifier for the criterion.
A label used to group criteria.
What this criterion measures.
The standard, competency, or skill this criterion connects to.
How much this criterion counts in scoring.
Display order of this criterion in the rubric.
When the criterion last changed.

objects.name: CASE Rubric Criterion Level
objects.plain_description: One performance level for a rubric criterion, such as beginning, developing, proficient, or advanced.
objects.why_it_exists: Levels let apps show score bands and feedback in a standards-aligned way.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this criterion level.
The criterion this level belongs to.
The official CASE UUID for the level.
A web identifier for the level.
The label for the performance level.
Explanation of the performance level.
fields.school_example: The learner explains the strategy and gives evidence.

fields.plain_description: Points awarded for this level.
Pre-written feedback or guidance for this level.
fields.school_example: Good explanation with minor missing detail.

fields.plain_description: Display order of this level.
When the level last changed.

objects.name: CASE API Status
objects.plain_description: Operational status information for failed or partial CASE service operations.
objects.why_it_exists: Importers and sync jobs need consistent status details when CASE operations do not fully succeed.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this status record.
The import, sync, or API operation this status belongs to.
Broad result of the CASE API request.
Whether the status is informational, warning, or error.
Human-readable details about the status.
More specific status details from the service.

case_version.label: CASE 1.1
case_version.plain_description: The CASE 1.1 framework and competency exchange model.

validation_status.label: Pending
validation_status.plain_description: The platform has not checked the package yet.

validation_status.label: Invalid
validation_status.plain_description: The package failed validation and should not be trusted for alignment.

validation_status.label: Warning
validation_status.plain_description: The package is usable but has non-blocking issues that should be reviewed.

framework_type.label: Competency framework
framework_type.plain_description: A framework of competencies or skills.

framework_type.label: Course codes
framework_type.plain_description: A framework used to publish course codes.

framework_type.label: Workforce skills
framework_type.plain_description: A framework of workforce skills or occupational competencies.

framework_type.label: Local framework
framework_type.plain_description: A district, school, or institution-specific framework.

adoption_status.label: Draft
adoption_status.plain_description: The framework is being developed and may change.

adoption_status.label: Adopted
adoption_status.plain_description: The framework is officially adopted or active.

adoption_status.label: Retired
adoption_status.plain_description: The framework is no longer active for new use.

adoption_status.label: Superseded
adoption_status.plain_description: The framework has been replaced by another version.

item_type.label: Domain
item_type.plain_description: A broad grouping inside a framework.

item_type.label: Strand
item_type.plain_description: A major learning strand or category.

item_type.label: Cluster
item_type.plain_description: A group of related standards or skills.

item_type.label: Standard
item_type.plain_description: A statement of what learners should know or do.

item_type.label: Competency
item_type.plain_description: A demonstrated capability or skill area.

item_type.label: Course code
item_type.plain_description: A course-code item in a CASE framework.

association_type.label: Is child of
association_type.plain_description: The origin item is a child of the destination item.

association_type.label: Is peer of
association_type.plain_description: The origin item is a peer of the destination item.

association_type.label: Is part of
association_type.plain_description: The origin item is included in the destination item.

association_type.label: Exact match of
association_type.plain_description: The origin item is equivalent to the destination item.

association_type.label: Precedes
association_type.plain_description: The origin item comes before the destination item.

association_type.label: Is related to
association_type.plain_description: The origin item is related to the destination item in a general way.

association_type.label: Replaced by
association_type.plain_description: The origin item has been replaced by the destination item.

association_type.label: Exemplar
association_type.plain_description: The destination is an example of the origin.

association_type.label: Has skill level
association_type.plain_description: The destination defines a skill or difficulty level for the origin.

association_type.label: Is translation of
association_type.plain_description: The destination is a translation of the origin.

target_type.label: CASE
target_type.plain_description: The link target points to a CASE framework object.

rubric_quality.label: Beginning
rubric_quality.plain_description: Early or limited evidence of the criterion.

rubric_quality.label: Developing
rubric_quality.plain_description: Partial or emerging evidence of the criterion.

rubric_quality.label: Advanced
rubric_quality.plain_description: Strong or extended evidence of the criterion.

code_major.label: Processing
code_major.plain_description: The operation is still being processed.

code_major.label: Unsupported
code_major.plain_description: The requested operation or feature is not supported.

unsupported_or_deferred.reason: Requires official conformance testing and certification process beyond generated dictionary artifacts.
Needs a hosted database, validation service, auth, and tenant/shared-reference policy before public import/search workflows are useful.
Extensions are preserved as governed metadata, but not promoted to core fields until intentionally modeled.
Version comparison and merge tooling should be implemented after persistent backend storage exists.
